records.ety: [OE. requeren, requiren, OF. requerre, F. requ<?/rir; L. pref. re- re- + quaerere to ask; cf. L. requirere. See Query, and cf. Request, Requisite.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Required (-kw?rd"); p. pr. & vb. n. Requiring.]

defs: To demand; to insist upon having; to claim as by right and authority; to exact; as, to require the surrender of property.
quotes: Shall I say to Caesar What you require of him?
By nature did what was by law required.

defs: To demand or exact as indispensable; to need.
quotes: Just gave what life required, and gave no more.
The two last [biographies] require to be particularly noticed.

defs: To ask as a favor; to request.
quotes: I was ashamed to require of the king a band of soldiers and horsemen to help us against the enemy in the way.
